blocks.content: But Shiira walked silently, staring at the ground.
Seeing her despondent, Shirone felt heavy-hearted.
If not for the accident in her childhood, she and Armin might have grown closer.
Yet he couldn't blame Armin for marrying. He had left Shiira's side to ease her guilt.
Nade grumbled, sounding displeased.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Tch. Relationships between men and women are really baffling.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What can you do? Not everything in the world goes as you wish.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Damn it! At school, she's so cold, but honestly, I didn't want to see her like this.
Nade clenched his fists in indignation, then quickened his pace to catch up to Shiira.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Teacher.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Huh? Yeah?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: We're hungry. Since we're out, how about treating us to a meal?
Shiira looked dumbfounded.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: You've got some nerve. In this situation, you're asking for a meal?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: What's the big deal? Even if we get punished, we still need to eat. We're learning magic to make a living anyway.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yeah, Teacher. Let's eat before heading back. I'm hungry too.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: In that case, I suggest we head to the noble district first. It's close to school, and I know a really delicious—and expensive—restaurant there.
Shiira lost even the will to get angry. Her students' nonchalant attitude was one thing, but she vaguely understood why they were acting this way.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Fine, alright. You must be tired from all the mischief, so let's eat first. But be prepared for what's waiting at school.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Hehe! Yes!
Nade winked at his friends.
While lifting Shiira's spirits was the primary goal, they also hoped to delay their return and mitigate the punishment.
The noble district was lined with luxurious shops. Iruki led Shiira to the most dazzling one.
Shirone gaped at the six-story building.
The entrance alone was extraordinary, befitting the nobles who frequented it.
Columns carved from entire white sharks supported the balcony ceiling, and marble statues of mythical beasts guarded the sides like sentinels.
Restaurant Gold Cupid.
The most high-end restaurant where every dish came with a premium.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wow! I've never been here either. Today's gonna be a real treat. Shirone, let's go in.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Uh, well…….
Shirone glanced at Shiira. As expected, she was staring at the sign with a sour smile.
It was understandable—this was the most expensive restaurant in the area.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Fine. Let's eat here.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Ooh! Our teacher! Your generosity is unmatched!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Foolish. It's a mistake to judge Teacher's generosity by this. A certified 6th-class mage can come and go from a place like this like it's a bathroom. Right, Teacher?

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Enough. Just get inside. But we're eating and going straight back to school.
For Shirone, who had never been to a noble-exclusive restaurant, the sheer scale inside was astonishing.
It was as if magic had been used—the interior felt completely different from the outside.
Tables were sparsely placed, almost wasteful, and one side featured a bar decorated with crystal lamps of all colors.
The patrons were, of course, nobles, and their jewelry alone made it clear they were among Creas's elite.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Shirone! Over here!
Shirone, lost in awe, headed to the table at his friends' call.
Nade and Iruki also glanced around occasionally but showed no signs of nervousness.
A waiter approached to take their order.
With a slick, swallow-tailed appearance, his gaze toward Shiira was uncomfortably sticky, making Shirone's group visibly displeased.

blocks.speaker: Waiter
blocks.content: Welcome, honored guests. Thank you for visiting Gold Cupid. Especially such a beautiful lady—I'm at a loss for words.
His flirting was one thing, but deliberately ignoring Shirone's group was blatant.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Tch. So this is the service at a high-class restaurant? How did they train their staff? Does he really think Teacher would be interested in a playboy like him?
Nade's anger simmered.
His eyes scanned the menu prices.
He'd order the most expensive dish to teach this waiter a lesson.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I'll have the full course.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: I'll have another course.
Shirone was still agonizing.
Honestly, he didn't recognize any of the dishes.
But he wasn't slow on the uptake, so he calmly ordered the same as Nade.
Something about Iruki's choices felt unreliable.
As the VIP dishes arrived one after another, the waiter's face grew pale.
His first thought was whether they could pay.
Every dish had a premium, but the difference between the cheapest and most expensive was over threefold.
If these kids had misunderstood, they might end up paying twelve times the expected price.
The waiter shot Shiira an uneasy glance, but she seemed unfazed as she chose her own meal.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: I'll have the Dasco course. And a bottle of Limunesse.
Shiira wasn't much of a drinker, but she occasionally drank cognac when feeling down.
Limunesse was a distilled liquor with a subtle vanilla aroma, expensive but popular among women for its smooth finish.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Huh? Teacher, you're drinking?

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Why not? You kids pick locks and break into houses, but I can't have a drink?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Haha. Fair point. Oh, right! You're off duty today, right?
Watching the waiter click his tongue and leave, Shirone's group exchanged smug looks.
But as reason returned, unease crept into their eyes.
Realizing they'd all ordered full courses, price tags flashed in their minds.
Only then did they worry if they'd gone too far, but Shiira seemed unbothered.
A certified 6th-class mage's income placed her in the top 10% of noble society.
Moreover, working at Alpheas Magic School, one of the kingdom's top five prestigious institutions, she could enjoy life without dipping into the Olipher faction's wealth.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Now that I think about it, she's really successful. Our teacher.
A life chasing dreams, not money.
But Shirone, the son of a mountain hunter, knew better than anyone how hard that was.
With four courses, food kept arriving nonstop.
Patrons at other tables glanced over with disdain.
Shirone and his friends ignored them, too busy trying to steal each other's food.
Though Nade and Iruki were nobles and Shirone had learned table manners, decorum was useless among friends.
Forks and knives flew across the table, but Shiira just watched them eat with a smile.
As their stomachs filled and the fun waned, they talked about magic, as befitting magic school students.
It was enjoyable and educational for Shiira too.
The laughter never stopped, and her gloomy mood lifted—proof that Nade's plan had worked.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Hehehe. Teacher, let this unworthy student offer you a drink.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Hmph! Think that'll make me go easy on you? I'm just letting it slide because we're eating. Be ready when we get back.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Sure, sure. Here you go.
Nade's charm shone even here.
Shiira didn't seem to mind, accepting the drinks he offered.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Teacher, aren't you drinking too much?

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Hehe, it's fine. This much is nothing.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Of course. A certified 6th-class mage wouldn't be fazed by mere alcohol, right?

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Really? You're desperate. But I'm really fine. I can hold my liquor.
Under Shirone's concerned gaze, Shiira downed another straight shot.
— One hour later —

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Yes. Teacher.
At the cleared table, Shiira leaned on her elbows, her face flushed as she swayed.
Meanwhile, Shirone's group sat stiffly, sweating.
The cognac bottle was empty in just an hour.
Shiira's speech was slurred, and her eyes slightly unfocused.
Shirone's group, unable to even turn their heads, exchanged glances with their eyes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We're in trouble. Teacher's drunk.
Shiira, who had been mumbling, suddenly slumped forward.
Nade hastily grabbed her arm before she fell off the chair.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Teacher, are you okay? We should head back now.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: You troublemaker!
Shiira jerked her head up and pinched Nade's cheek mercilessly.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Owowow! That hurts!

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Listen to me properly. I want to be a kind teacher like Etella. Focus on your studies, alright?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Yes, yes! Got it, Teacher! It really hurts!

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: And Shirone, Iruki. You two!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Yes!

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: You're no different. Don't slack off just because you're talented. I won't forgive that. Think of me as a bad teacher if you want. I won't forgive you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No one thinks you're bad. So please, snap out of it.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Huuu. Yeah, I know. Why do I put myself through this? Ugh.
Shiira's eyelids drooped, and her head lolled.
Finally, she buried her face in her arms on the table.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Sleepy. Gonna sleep.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Teacher? We haven't even paid yet.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Sleepy. Gonna sleep.
No response.
Nade looked dumbfounded at his friends.
But they had no solutions either.
As they racked their brains to escape the predicament, two men from a nearby table approached.

blocks.speaker: Man
blocks.content: The young lady seems quite drunk. Shall we call a carriage?
A well-dressed youth spoke to Shiira.
Behind him, a burly man smirked.
Shirone's group's eyes turned icy.
They'd noticed earlier—these two had been eyeing Shiira and making lewd comments.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Teacher? Teacher. Snap out of it.
The best plan was to wake Shiira and leave quickly.
But as soon as she sat up, she slumped back onto the table.
Nade's use of 'Teacher' caught the man's attention, and he pressed closer.
